steps.title: Sketch the stroller frame (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch the stroller's basic triangle: a handlebar across the top, two legs angling forward and down to the front wheel axle, and a shorter rear leg dropping straight down beneath the handle. Think of a sideways letter A.

steps.title: Add the seat and canopy (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a rectangle within the frame for the seat/body of the pram. Above it, draw the canopy as a curved arc — like a rounded shade hood — attached to the upper frame.

steps.title: Add the wheels (pencil)
steps.description: Place a circle or slightly flattened oval at the front axle and at the base of the rear leg. The front wheel is below the seat; the rear wheel sits directly below the handle.

steps.title: Sketch the figure behind (pencil)
steps.description: Stand the person behind the stroller with hands on the handlebar. Their upper body and head are visible above the handle; the lower body is partially hidden behind the frame. Rough in their torso, arms, and head only.

steps.title: Check the overlap (pencil)
steps.description: Trace where the figure's legs and lower body are hidden by the stroller frame. If a line from the person would pass behind the frame, don't draw it — the hidden parts are genuinely hidden.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the stroller frame
steps.description: Switch to ink. Ink the stroller: handlebar, the two main legs, the seat rectangle. Use clean straight lines where the frame is rigid metal — wobbles here will look like a bent frame, not artistic style.

steps.title: Ink the canopy, wheels, and figure (ink)
steps.description: Ink the canopy with a smooth arc, then the wheels as clean circles. Finally, ink the figure — head, torso, and arms. Where the figure is behind the stroller frame, stop the figure's outline at the frame edge and restart it on the other side. The figure peeks out; the stroller is in front.
